content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a p-code VM and p-code assembler (pasm) for the COR24 24-bit RISC ISA. The VM is a stack-based interpreter written in COR24 assembly. The pasm tool (also COR24 assembly) assembles human-readable .pasm source into bytecode for testing the VM. Pascal compiler is a separate project.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-vm-scaffold [production]: Create pvm.s scaffold: COR24 entry point, VM state struct (pc, esp, csp, fp, ...
  [x] 002-vm-stack-ops [legacy]: Implement push/push_s/dup/drop/swap/over opcode handlers and sys PUTC (opcode 0x60 with id=1). Test by embedding hardcoded bytecode: push_s 72, sys 1, push_s 10, sys 1, halt — should print H-newline to UART.
  [x] 003-vm-arithmetic [legacy]: Implement add/sub/mul/div/mod/neg/and/or/xor/not/shl/shr and eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge opcode handlers. Test with hardcoded bytecode: push 7, push 6, mul, sys PUTC should output ASCII '*' (42).
  [x] 004-vm-control-flow [legacy]: Implement jmp/jz/jnz opcode handlers. Test with hardcoded countdown loop: push 5, loop: dup, jz done, push_s 1, sub, jmp loop, done: halt.
  [x] 005-vm-frames [legacy]: Implement call/ret/enter/leave and loadl/storel/loada/storea opcode handlers. Build frame management: push/pop activation records on call stack, argument passing from eval stack to frame. Test with hardcoded bytecode for a factorial function.
  [x] 006-vm-memory [legacy]: Implement loadg/storeg/addrg/addrl and indirect load/store/loadb/storeb. Implement globals segment. Test with hardcoded bytecode: global counter, store/load/print.
  [x] 007-vm-nonlocal [legacy]: Implement loadn/storen for static link chain traversal (needed for Pascal nested procedures). Test with hardcoded 2-level nested procedure accessing outer local.
  [ ] 008-vm-syscalls [legacy]: Implement remaining sys calls: HALT(0), GETC(2), LED(3), ALLOC(4), FREE(5). Bump allocator for heap. Test: LED toggle, UART echo, heap alloc/write/read. <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 009-vm-traps [legacy]: Implement trap handling for all trap codes (div-zero, stack overflow/underflow, invalid opcode, invalid address, nil pointer). Print diagnostic to UART on trap. Test each trap condition.
  [ ] 010-pasm-lexer [legacy]: Create pasm.s — p-code assembler in COR24 assembly. Start with lexer: read pasm source from UART, tokenize into token types (mnemonic, number, label, directive, newline, comment, eof). Use a token buffer in memory. Test by feeding simple pasm source and dumping token stream.
  [ ] 011-pasm-parser [legacy]: Implement pasm parser: two-pass assembly. Pass 1: collect labels and procedure addresses, count globals/data sizes. Pass 2: emit bytecode. Handle .const/.global/.data/.proc/.end directives. Test by assembling a minimal program (push 42, halt) and verifying bytecode bytes.
  [ ] 012-pasm-integration [legacy]: Connect pasm output to VM input. Assemble pasm source into bytecode buffer, then execute. Test with hello.pasm (full puts procedure, data string, main calling puts). First end-to-end test.
  [ ] 013-test-suite [legacy]: Create full test suite: tests/t01-arith.pasm through t10-traps.pasm. Create demo.sh test harness with golden output comparison (following tf24a pattern). Create examples/hello.pasm, examples/blink.pasm, examples/echo.pasm.
  [ ] 014-hardening [legacy]: Fix bugs found by test suite. Add stack depth checking. Validate memory bounds. Clean up diagnostic output. Ensure all tests pass. Final documentation update.

=== CURRENT STEP: 008-vm-syscalls [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Implement remaining sys calls: HALT(0), GETC(2), LED(3), ALLOC(4), FREE(5). Bump allocator for heap. Test: LED toggle, UART echo, heap alloc/write/read.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.content:
1→# pv24a — Design Document
     2→
     3→## 1. VM State
     4→
     5→The p-code VM maintains the following state in a memory struct:
     6→
     7→```
     8→vm_state:
     9→    .word 0     ; pc   — p-code instruction pointer
    10→    .word 0     ; esp  — eval stack pointer (top of eval stack)
    11→    .word 0     ; csp  — call stack pointer (top of call stack)
    12→    .word 0     ; fp   — current frame pointer (call stack)
    13→    .word 0     ; gp   — globals base address
    14→    .word 0     ; hp   — heap pointer (next free)
    15→    .word 0     ; code — base address of p-code segment
    16→    .word 0     ; status — 0=running, 1=halted, 2=trapped
    17→    .word 0     ; trap_code — last trap reason
    18→```
    19→
    20→## 2. P-Code Instruction Set
    21→
    22→### 2.1 Stack / Constants
    23→
    24→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    25→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    26→| `push` imm24 | 4 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push 24-bit immediate |
    27→| `push_s` imm8 | 2 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push sign-extended 8-bit immediate |
    28→| `dup` | 1 byte | ( a -- a a ) | Duplicate top |
    29→| `drop` | 1 byte | ( a -- ) | Discard top |
    30→| `swap` | 1 byte | ( a b -- b a ) | Swap top two |
    31→| `over` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a b a ) | Copy second to top |
    32→
    33→### 2.2 Arithmetic / Logic
    34→
    35→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    36→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    37→| `add` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a+b ) | Add |
    38→| `sub` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a-b ) | Subtract |
    39→| `mul` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a*b ) | Multiply |
    40→| `div` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a/b ) | Divide (trap on b=0) |
    41→| `mod` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a%b ) | Modulo (trap on b=0) |
    42→| `neg` | 1 byte | ( a -- -a ) | Negate |
    43→| `and` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a&b ) | Bitwise AND |
    44→| `or` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a\|b ) | Bitwise OR |
    45→| `xor` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a^b ) | Bitwise XOR |
    46→| `not` | 1 byte | ( a -- ~a ) | Bitwise NOT |
    47→| `shl` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a<<n ) | Shift left |
    48→| `shr` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a>>n ) | Arithmetic shift right |
    49→
    50→### 2.3 Comparison
    51→
    52→All comparisons push 1 (true) or 0 (false):
    53→
    54→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    55→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    56→| `eq` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Equal |
    57→| `ne` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Not equal |
    58→| `lt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less than (signed) |
    59→| `le` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less or equal (signed) |
    60→| `gt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater than (signed) |
    61→| `ge` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater or equal (signed) |
    62→
    63→### 2.4 Control Flow
    64→
    65→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    66→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    67→| `jmp` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( -- ) | Unconditional jump |
    68→| `jz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if zero |
    69→| `jnz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if nonzero |
    70→| `call` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( args... -- ) | Call procedure (static link = 0) |
    71→| `calln` depth8 addr24 | 5 bytes | ( args... -- ) | Call with static link (depth=0: nested, depth=N: N levels up) |
    72→| `ret` nargs8 | 2 bytes | ( [retval] -- [retval] ) | Return, clean nargs |
    73→| `halt` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Stop VM |
    74→| `trap` code8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Trigger trap |
    75→
    76→### 2.5 Local / Global / Nonlocal Access
    77→
    78→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    79→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    80→| `enter` nlocals8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Set up frame, reserve N local slots |
    81→| `leave` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Tear down frame |
    82→| `loadl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load local at offset |
    83→| `storel` off8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store local at offset |
    84→| `loadg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load global at offset |
    85→| `storeg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store global at offset |
    86→| `addrl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of local |
    87→| `addrg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of global |
    88→| `loadn` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load nonlocal via static link chain |
    89→| `storen` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store nonlocal via static link chain |
    90→
    91→### 2.6 Indirect Memory Access
    92→
    93→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    94→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    95→| `load` | 1 byte | ( addr -- val ) | Load word from address |
    96→| `store` | 1 byte | ( val addr -- ) | Store word to address |
    97→| `loadb` | 1 byte | ( addr -- byte ) | Load byte (zero-extended) |
    98→| `storeb` | 1 byte | ( byte addr -- ) | Store byte |
    99→
   100→### 2.7 System / I/O
   101→
   102→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
   103→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
   104→| `sys` id8 | 2 bytes | (varies) | System call |
   105→
   106→System call IDs:
   107→
   108→| ID | Name | Stack Effect | Description |
   109→|----|------|-------------|-------------|
   110→| 0 | HALT | ( -- ) | Stop execution |
   111→| 1 | PUTC | ( char -- ) | Write byte to UART |
   112→| 2 | GETC | ( -- char ) | Read byte from UART (blocking) |
   113→| 3 | LED | ( state -- ) | Write LED state |
   114→| 4 | ALLOC | ( size -- ptr ) | Bump-allocate heap block |
   115→| 5 | FREE | ( ptr -- ) | Free heap block (no-op in bump mode) |
   116→
   117→### 2.8 Argument Access
   118→
   119→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
   120→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
   121→| `loada` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load argument by index |
   122→| `storea` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store argument by index |
   123→
   124→## 3. Call Frame Layout
   125→
   126→When `call` executes, a new frame is pushed onto the call stack:
   127→
   128→```
   129→         ┌──────────────────┐  ← new csp (after enter)
   130→         │ local[N-1]       │  offset = +(N-1)*3 from fp
   131→         │ ...              │
   132→         │ local[0]         │  offset = 0 from fp
   133→fp ───→  ├──────────────────┤
   134→         │ procedure ID     │  offset = -3
   135→         │ static link      │  offset = -6  (lexically enclosing frame)
   136→         │ dynamic link     │  offset = -9  (caller's fp)
   137→         │ return PC        │  offset = -12
   138→         ├──────────────────┤
   139→         │ arg[N-1]         │  offset = -15  (from fp)
   140→         │ ...              │
   141→         │ arg[0]           │  offset = -15 - (N-1)*3
   142→         └──────────────────┘
   143→```
   144→
   145→- **Caller** pushes arguments onto eval stack, then `call addr`
   146→- **call** instruction: pops args from eval stack into call stack frame header,
   147→  pushes return PC, dynamic link, static link, proc ID
   148→- **enter N**: advances csp by N*3 to reserve local slots, sets fp
   149→- **leave**: restores fp to dynamic link, restores csp
   150→- **ret N**: pushes return value (if any) to eval stack, pops frame, cleans N args
   151→
   152→## 4. Calling Convention
   153→
   154→Simple and explicit:
   155→
   156→1. Caller pushes arguments left-to-right onto eval stack
   157→2. Caller executes `call <proc_addr>`
   158→3. Callee executes `enter <nlocals>` (reserves local slots)
   159→4. Callee accesses args via `loada 0`, `loada 1`, etc.
   160→5. Callee accesses locals via `loadl 0`, `loadl 1`, etc.
   161→6. For functions: callee pushes return value onto eval stack
   162→7. Callee executes `ret <nargs>` (cleans args from frame)
   163→8. Caller finds return value (if any) on eval stack
   164→
   165→## 5. P-Code Assembler (pasm) Syntax
   166→
   167→### 5.0 File Extensions
   168→
   169→- `.spc` — p-code assembler source files (input to pasm)
   170→- `.p24` — assembled p-code bytecode files (output of pasm, input to VM)
   171→
   172→### 5.1 Source Format
   173→
   174→```
   175→; Comment (semicolon to end of line)
   176→
   177→.const UART_DATA  -65280      ; named constant
   178→.const UART_STATUS -65279
   179→.const LED_PORT   -65024
   180→
   181→.global counter 1             ; 1 word of global storage
   182→.global buffer 32             ; 32 words
   183→
   184→.data msg 72, 101, 108, 108, 111, 0   ; "Hello\0" as bytes
   185→
   186→.proc main 0                 ; procedure, 0 locals
   187→    push msg                  ; push address of msg
   188→    call puts
   189→    halt
   190→.end
   191→
   192→.proc puts 1                 ; 1 local (loop pointer)
   193→    loada 0                   ; arg0 = string address
   194→    storel 0                  ; local0 = current ptr
   195→loop:
   196→    loadl 0
   197→    loadb                     ; load byte at ptr
   198→    dup
   199→    jz done                   ; if zero, done
   200→    sys 1                     ; PUTC
   201→    loadl 0
   202→    push 1
   203→    add
   204→    storel 0                  ; ptr++
   205→    jmp loop
   206→done:
   207→    drop                      ; drop the zero
   208→    ret 1                     ; return, clean 1 arg
   209→.end
   210→```
   211→
   212→### 5.2 Directives
   213→
   214→| Directive | Syntax | Description |
   215→|-----------|--------|-------------|
   216→| `.const` | `.const NAME value` | Define named constant |
   217→| `.global` | `.global NAME nwords` | Reserve global storage |
   218→| `.data` | `.data NAME byte, byte, ...` | Define byte data (string/array) |
   219→| `.proc` | `.proc NAME nlocals` | Begin procedure (nlocals = local slot count) |
   220→| `.end` | `.end` | End procedure |
   221→
   222→### 5.3 Labels
   223→
   224→Labels appear on their own line, ending with `:`:
   225→
   226→```
   227→loop:
   228→    ; instructions...
   229→    jmp loop
   230→```
   231→
   232→Labels are local to the enclosing `.proc` / `.end` block.
   233→
   234→### 5.4 Integer Literals
   235→
   236→- Decimal: `42`, `-1`, `0`
   237→- Hex: `0xFF` (if supported) or decimal equivalents
   238→- Named constants: `UART_DATA` (substituted by assembler)
   239→
   240→### 5.5 Instructions
   241→
   242→One instruction per line. Operand follows mnemonic separated by whitespace:
   243→
   244→```
   245→push 42
   246→loadl 0
   247→jz done
   248→call puts
   249→sys 1
   250→ret 0
   251→```
   252→
   253→## 6. Opcode Encoding Table
   254→
   255→Opcodes are assigned sequential byte values:
   256→
   257→| Byte | Mnemonic | Operand |
   258→|------|----------|---------|
   259→| 0x00 | halt | — |
   260→| 0x01 | push | imm24 |
   261→| 0x02 | push_s | imm8 |
   262→| 0x03 | dup | — |
   263→| 0x04 | drop | — |
   264→| 0x05 | swap | — |
   265→| 0x06 | over | — |
   266→| 0x10 | add | — |
   267→| 0x11 | sub | — |
   268→| 0x12 | mul | — |
   269→| 0x13 | div | — |
   270→| 0x14 | mod | — |
   271→| 0x15 | neg | — |
   272→| 0x16 | and | — |
   273→| 0x17 | or | — |
   274→| 0x18 | xor | — |
   275→| 0x19 | not | — |
   276→| 0x1A | shl | — |
   277→| 0x1B | shr | — |
   278→| 0x20 | eq | — |
   279→| 0x21 | ne | — |
   280→| 0x22 | lt | — |
   281→| 0x23 | le | — |
   282→| 0x24 | gt | — |
   283→| 0x25 | ge | — |
   284→| 0x30 | jmp | addr24 |
   285→| 0x31 | jz | addr24 |
   286→| 0x32 | jnz | addr24 |
   287→| 0x33 | call | addr24 |
   288→| 0x34 | ret | nargs8 |
   289→| 0x35 | calln | depth8 addr24 |
   290→| 0x36 | trap | code8 |
   291→| 0x40 | enter | nlocals8 |
   292→| 0x41 | leave | — |
   293→| 0x42 | loadl | off8 |
   294→| 0x43 | storel | off8 |
   295→| 0x44 | loadg | off24 |
   296→| 0x45 | storeg | off24 |
   297→| 0x46 | addrl | off8 |
   298→| 0x47 | addrg | off24 |
   299→| 0x48 | loada | idx8 |
   300→| 0x49 | storea | idx8 |
   301→| 0x4A | loadn | depth8 off8 |
   302→| 0x4B | storen | depth8 off8 |
   303→| 0x50 | load | — |
   304→| 0x51 | store | — |
   305→| 0x52 | loadb | — |
   306→| 0x53 | storeb | — |
   307→| 0x60 | sys | id8 |
   308→
   309→Reserved ranges 0x70–0xFF for future opcodes (heap, debug, extended ops).
   310→
   311→## 7. Trap Codes
   312→
   313→| Code | Name | Trigger |
   314→|------|------|---------|
   315→| 0 | USER_TRAP | `trap 0` instruction |
   316→| 1 | DIV_ZERO | Division/modulo by zero |
   317→| 2 | STACK_OVERFLOW | Eval or call stack exceeds limit |
   318→| 3 | STACK_UNDERFLOW | Eval stack pop when empty |
   319→| 4 | INVALID_OPCODE | Unknown opcode byte |
   320→| 5 | INVALID_ADDRESS | Memory access out of bounds |
   321→| 6 | NIL_POINTER | Dereference of address 0 |
   322→| 7 | BOUNDS_CHECK | Array index out of bounds |
   323→
   324→On trap: VM sets status=2, trap_code=N, halts. Diagnostic message to UART.
   325→
   326→## 8. Memory Segment Layout
   327→
   328→At VM initialization:
   329→
   330→1. **Code segment** starts at a fixed base address (after VM code)
   331→2. **Constants/data** follows code (emitted by pasm)
   332→3. **Globals** follow data (allocated per `.global` directives)
   333→4. **Call stack** starts after globals, grows upward
   334→5. **Eval stack** starts after call stack region, grows upward
   335→6. **Heap** starts after eval stack region, grows upward
   336→7. Segment base addresses stored in VM state struct
   337→
   338→Sizes are configurable at VM init time. Default allocations:
   339→- Call stack: 4 KB
   340→- Eval stack: 2 KB
   341→- Heap: remaining SRAM up to ~900 KB
   342→
   343→## 9. Implementation Strategy
   344→
   345→### Phase 1: Minimal VM
   346→- Opcode dispatch loop
   347→- push, dup, drop, add, sub, halt
   348→- UART output via sys PUTC
   349→- Hardcoded test bytecode (no pasm yet)
   350→
   351→### Phase 2: Control Flow + Procedures
   352→- jmp, jz, jnz
   353→- call, ret, enter, leave
   354→- loadl, storel, loada, storea
   355→- Frame management
   356→
   357→### Phase 3: Full Instruction Set
   358→- All arithmetic/logic/comparison opcodes
   359→- Globals (loadg, storeg)
   360→- Indirect memory (load, store, loadb, storeb)
   361→- Nonlocal access (loadn, storen)
   362→- All sys calls
   363→
   364→### Phase 4: P-Code Assembler
   365→- Lexer for pasm syntax (UART input)
   366→- Two-pass assembly (collect labels, emit bytes)
   367→- Directive handling (.const, .global, .data, .proc/.end)
   368→- Output bytecode to memory buffer
   369→
   370→### Phase 5: Integration & Test Suite
   371→- pasm assembles test programs
   372→- VM executes assembled bytecode
   373→- Regression test harness (demo.sh)
   374→- Full test coverage per staged plan
   375→
   376→## 10. COR24 Assembly Patterns
   377→
   378→Reference patterns from tf24a for common idioms:
   379→
   380→### Dispatch Table
   381→```asm

# pv24a — Design Document

## 1. VM State

The p-code VM maintains the following state in a memory struct:

```
vm_state:
    .word 0     ; pc   — p-code instruction pointer
    .word 0     ; esp  — eval stack pointer (top of eval stack)
    .word 0     ; csp  — call stack pointer (top of call stack)
    .word 0     ; fp   — current frame pointer (call stack)
    .word 0     ; gp   — globals base address
    .word 0     ; hp   — heap pointer (next free)
    .word 0     ; code — base address of p-code segment
    .word 0     ; status — 0=running, 1=halted, 2=trapped
    .word 0     ; trap_code — last trap reason
```

## 2. P-Code Instruction Set

### 2.1 Stack / Constants

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `push` imm24 | 4 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push 24-bit immediate |
| `push_s` imm8 | 2 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push sign-extended 8-bit immediate |
| `dup` | 1 byte | ( a -- a a ) | Duplicate top |
| `drop` | 1 byte | ( a -- ) | Discard top |
| `swap` | 1 byte | ( a b -- b a ) | Swap top two |
| `over` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a b a ) | Copy second to top |

### 2.2 Arithmetic / Logic

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `add` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a+b ) | Add |
| `sub` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a-b ) | Subtract |
| `mul` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a*b ) | Multiply |
| `div` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a/b ) | Divide (trap on b=0) |
| `mod` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a%b ) | Modulo (trap on b=0) |
| `neg` | 1 byte | ( a -- -a ) | Negate |
| `and` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a&b ) | Bitwise AND |
| `or` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a\|b ) | Bitwise OR |
| `xor` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a^b ) | Bitwise XOR |
| `not` | 1 byte | ( a -- ~a ) | Bitwise NOT |
| `shl` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a<<n ) | Shift left |
| `shr` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a>>n ) | Arithmetic shift right |

### 2.3 Comparison

All comparisons push 1 (true) or 0 (false):

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `eq` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Equal |
| `ne` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Not equal |
| `lt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less than (signed) |
| `le` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less or equal (signed) |
| `gt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater than (signed) |
| `ge` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater or equal (signed) |

### 2.4 Control Flow

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `jmp` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( -- ) | Unconditional jump |
| `jz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if zero |
| `jnz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if nonzero |
| `call` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( args... -- ) | Call procedure (static link = 0) |
| `calln` depth8 addr24 | 5 bytes | ( args... -- ) | Call with static link (depth=0: nested, depth=N: N levels up) |
| `ret` nargs8 | 2 bytes | ( [retval] -- [retval] ) | Return, clean nargs |
| `halt` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Stop VM |
| `trap` code8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Trigger trap |

### 2.5 Local / Global / Nonlocal Access

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `enter` nlocals8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Set up frame, reserve N local slots |
| `leave` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Tear down frame |
| `loadl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load local at offset |
| `storel` off8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store local at offset |
| `loadg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load global at offset |
| `storeg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store global at offset |
| `addrl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of local |
| `addrg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of global |
| `loadn` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load nonlocal via static link chain |
| `storen` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store nonlocal via static link chain |

### 2.6 Indirect Memory Access

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `load` | 1 byte | ( addr -- val ) | Load word from address |
| `store` | 1 byte | ( val addr -- ) | Store word to address |
| `loadb` | 1 byte | ( addr -- byte ) | Load byte (zero-extended) |
| `storeb` | 1 byte | ( byte addr -- ) | Store byte |

### 2.7 System / I/O

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `sys` id8 | 2 bytes | (varies) | System call |

System call IDs:

| ID | Name | Stack Effect | Description |
|----|------|-------------|-------------|
| 0 | HALT | ( -- ) | Stop execution |
| 1 | PUTC | ( char -- ) | Write byte to UART |
| 2 | GETC | ( -- char ) | Read byte from UART (blocking) |
| 3 | LED | ( state -- ) | Write LED state |
| 4 | ALLOC | ( size -- ptr ) | Bump-allocate heap block |
| 5 | FREE | ( ptr -- ) | Free heap block (no-op in bump mode) |

### 2.8 Argument Access

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `loada` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load argument by index |
| `storea` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store argument by index |

## 3. Call Frame Layout

When `call` executes, a new frame is pushed onto the call stack:

```
         ┌──────────────────┐  ← new csp (after enter)
         │ local[N-1]       │  offset = +(N-1)*3 from fp
         │ ...              │
         │ local[0]         │  offset = 0 from fp
fp ───→  ├──────────────────┤
         │ procedure ID     │  offset = -3
         │ static link      │  offset = -6  (lexically enclosing frame)
         │ dynamic link     │  offset = -9  (caller's fp)
         │ return PC        │  offset = -12
         ├──────────────────┤
         │ arg[N-1]         │  offset = -15  (from fp)
         │ ...              │
         │ arg[0]           │  offset = -15 - (N-1)*3
         └──────────────────┘
```

- **Caller** pushes arguments onto eval stack, then `call addr`
- **call** instruction: pops args from eval stack into call stack frame header,
  pushes return PC, dynamic link, static link, proc ID
- **enter N**: advances csp by N*3 to reserve local slots, sets fp
- **leave**: restores fp to dynamic link, restores csp
- **ret N**: pushes return value (if any) to eval stack, pops frame, cleans N args

## 4. Calling Convention

Simple and explicit:

1. Caller pushes arguments left-to-right onto eval stack
2. Caller executes `call <proc_addr>`
3. Callee executes `enter <nlocals>` (reserves local slots)
4. Callee accesses args via `loada 0`, `loada 1`, etc.
5. Callee accesses locals via `loadl 0`, `loadl 1`, etc.
6. For functions: callee pushes return value onto eval stack
7. Callee executes `ret <nargs>` (cleans args from frame)
8. Caller finds return value (if any) on eval stack

## 5. P-Code Assembler (pasm) Syntax

### 5.0 File Extensions

- `.spc` — p-code assembler source files (input to pasm)
- `.p24` — assembled p-code bytecode files (output of pasm, input to VM)

### 5.1 Source Format

```
; Comment (semicolon to end of line)

.const UART_DATA  -65280      ; named constant
.const UART_STATUS -65279
.const LED_PORT   -65024

.global counter 1             ; 1 word of global storage
.global buffer 32             ; 32 words

.data msg 72, 101, 108, 108, 111, 0   ; "Hello\0" as bytes

.proc main 0                 ; procedure, 0 locals
    push msg                  ; push address of msg
    call puts
    halt
.end

.proc puts 1                 ; 1 local (loop pointer)
    loada 0                   ; arg0 = string address
    storel 0                  ; local0 = current ptr
loop:
    loadl 0
    loadb                     ; load byte at ptr
    dup
    jz done                   ; if zero, done
    sys 1                     ; PUTC
    loadl 0
    push 1
    add
    storel 0                  ; ptr++
    jmp loop
done:
    drop                      ; drop the zero
    ret 1                     ; return, clean 1 arg
.end
```

### 5.2 Directives

| Directive | Syntax | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `.const` | `.const NAME value` | Define named constant |
| `.global` | `.global NAME nwords` | Reserve global storage |
| `.data` | `.data NAME byte, byte, ...` | Define byte data (string/array) |
| `.proc` | `.proc NAME nlocals` | Begin procedure (nlocals = local slot count) |
| `.end` | `.end` | End procedure |

### 5.3 Labels

Labels appear on their own line, ending with `:`:

```
loop:
    ; instructions...
    jmp loop
```

Labels are local to the enclosing `.proc` / `.end` block.

### 5.4 Integer Literals

- Decimal: `42`, `-1`, `0`
- Hex: `0xFF` (if supported) or decimal equivalents
- Named constants: `UART_DATA` (substituted by assembler)

### 5.5 Instructions

One instruction per line. Operand follows mnemonic separated by whitespace:

```
push 42
loadl 0
jz done
call puts
sys 1
ret 0
```

## 6. Opcode Encoding Table

Opcodes are assigned sequential byte values:

| Byte | Mnemonic | Operand |
|------|----------|---------|
| 0x00 | halt | — |
| 0x01 | push | imm24 |
| 0x02 | push_s | imm8 |
| 0x03 | dup | — |
| 0x04 | drop | — |
| 0x05 | swap | — |
| 0x06 | over | — |
| 0x10 | add | — |
| 0x11 | sub | — |
| 0x12 | mul | — |
| 0x13 | div | — |
| 0x14 | mod | — |
| 0x15 | neg | — |
| 0x16 | and | — |
| 0x17 | or | — |
| 0x18 | xor | — |
| 0x19 | not | — |
| 0x1A | shl | — |
| 0x1B | shr | — |
| 0x20 | eq | — |
| 0x21 | ne | — |
| 0x22 | lt | — |
| 0x23 | le | — |
| 0x24 | gt | — |
| 0x25 | ge | — |
| 0x30 | jmp | addr24 |
| 0x31 | jz | addr24 |
| 0x32 | jnz | addr24 |
| 0x33 | call | addr24 |
| 0x34 | ret | nargs8 |
| 0x35 | calln | depth8 addr24 |
| 0x36 | trap | code8 |
| 0x40 | enter | nlocals8 |
| 0x41 | leave | — |
| 0x42 | loadl | off8 |
| 0x43 | storel | off8 |
| 0x44 | loadg | off24 |
| 0x45 | storeg | off24 |
| 0x46 | addrl | off8 |
| 0x47 | addrg | off24 |
| 0x48 | loada | idx8 |
| 0x49 | storea | idx8 |
| 0x4A | loadn | depth8 off8 |
| 0x4B | storen | depth8 off8 |
| 0x50 | load | — |
| 0x51 | store | — |
| 0x52 | loadb | — |
| 0x53 | storeb | — |
| 0x60 | sys | id8 |

Reserved ranges 0x70–0xFF for future opcodes (heap, debug, extended ops).

## 7. Trap Codes

| Code | Name | Trigger |
|------|------|---------|
| 0 | USER_TRAP | `trap 0` instruction |
| 1 | DIV_ZERO | Division/modulo by zero |
| 2 | STACK_OVERFLOW | Eval or call stack exceeds limit |
| 3 | STACK_UNDERFLOW | Eval stack pop when empty |
| 4 | INVALID_OPCODE | Unknown opcode byte |
| 5 | INVALID_ADDRESS | Memory access out of bounds |
| 6 | NIL_POINTER | Dereference of address 0 |
| 7 | BOUNDS_CHECK | Array index out of bounds |

On trap: VM sets status=2, trap_code=N, halts. Diagnostic message to UART.

## 8. Memory Segment Layout

At VM initialization:

1. **Code segment** starts at a fixed base address (after VM code)
2. **Constants/data** follows code (emitted by pasm)
3. **Globals** follow data (allocated per `.global` directives)
4. **Call stack** starts after globals, grows upward
5. **Eval stack** starts after call stack region, grows upward
6. **Heap** starts after eval stack region, grows upward
7. Segment base addresses stored in VM state struct

Sizes are configurable at VM init time. Default allocations:
- Call stack: 4 KB
- Eval stack: 2 KB
- Heap: remaining SRAM up to ~900 KB

## 9. Implementation Strategy

### Phase 1: Minimal VM
- Opcode dispatch loop
- push, dup, drop, add, sub, halt
- UART output via sys PUTC
- Hardcoded test bytecode (no pasm yet)

### Phase 2: Control Flow + Procedures
- jmp, jz, jnz
- call, ret, enter, leave
- loadl, storel, loada, storea
- Frame management

### Phase 3: Full Instruction Set
- All arithmetic/logic/comparison opcodes
- Globals (loadg, storeg)
- Indirect memory (load, store, loadb, storeb)
- Nonlocal access (loadn, storen)
- All sys calls

### Phase 4: P-Code Assembler
- Lexer for pasm syntax (UART input)
- Two-pass assembly (collect labels, emit bytes)
- Directive handling (.const, .global, .data, .proc/.end)
- Output bytecode to memory buffer

### Phase 5: Integration & Test Suite
- pasm assembles test programs
- VM executes assembled bytecode
- Regression test harness (demo.sh)
- Full test coverage per staged plan

## 10. COR24 Assembly Patterns

Reference patterns from tf24a for common idioms:

### Dispatch Table
```asm
; Index into jump table by opcode byte
; r0 = opcode
la r2, dispatch_table
; multiply r0 by 3 (entry size = .word = 3 bytes)
; ... load target address, jmp
```

### UART Output
```asm
; r0 = character to send
la r2, -65280       ; UART base
tx_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)   ; status (sign-extended)
    cls r0, z       ; C = (status < 0) = TX busy
    brt tx_wait
    sb r0, 0(r2)   ; write byte
```

### Memory Struct Access
```asm
; Load vm_state.pc into r0
la fp, vm_state
lw r0, 0(fp)       ; pc at offset 0
```

file.content:
pop r2
    ; r2 = addr
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x60 — sys id8: system call dispatch
op_sys:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    ; Fetch sys ID byte
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    lbu r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = sys id
    ; Increment pc
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Dispatch on sys id
    mov r0, r2
    lc r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2
    brt sys_putc
    ; Unknown sys id — trap
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)

; sys PUTC (id=1): pop char from eval stack, send to UART
sys_putc:
    ; fp = &vm_state
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    add r2, -3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = new esp = &TOS
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = char value
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
sys_putc_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt sys_putc_wait
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; Invalid opcode: set status=2, trap_code=4 (INVALID_OPCODE)
op_invalid:
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lc r0, 2
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    lc r0, 4
    sw r0, 24(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; Stub handler — all unimplemented opcodes trap as invalid
op_stub:
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; Dispatch table (97 entries: opcodes 0x00 through 0x60)
; Each entry is a .word (3 bytes) holding the handler address
; ============================================================
dispatch_table:
    ; 0x00-0x06: Stack / Constants
    .word op_halt
    .word op_push
    .word op_push_s
    .word op_dup
    .word op_drop
    .word op_swap
    .word op_over
    ; 0x07-0x0F: reserved (gap)
    .word op_invalid

1→; pvm.s — P-Code Virtual Machine for COR24
     2→;
     3→; Register allocation:
     4→;   r0 = W (work/scratch, opcode dispatch)
     5→;   r1 = scratch / return address for jal calls
     6→;   r2 = scratch
     7→;   sp = COR24 hardware stack (EBR, used sparingly)
     8→;   fp = memory base for indexed loads/stores
     9→;
    10→; VM state lives in memory struct (vm_state) because COR24 has only 3 GPRs.
    11→;
    12→; UART: data at -65280 (0xFF0100), status at -65279 (0xFF0101)
    13→;   TX busy = status bit 7 (sign bit via lb sign-extend)
    14→;   RX ready = status bit 0
    15→; LED: port at -65024 (0xFF0200)
    16→;
    17→; COR24 ISA notes:
    18→;   lbu = load byte zero-extend, lb = load byte sign-extend
    19→;   ceq ra, rb sets C if ra == rb; cls ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (signed)
    20→;   clu ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (unsigned)
    21→;   brt/brf = branch if C true/false; bra = branch always
    22→;   jal r1, (r2) = r1 = PC+1, PC = r2 (call convention)
    23→;   jmp (r1) = return from jal call
    24→;   Valid load/store base registers: r0, r1, r2, fp (NOT sp)
    25→;   Can push/pop: r0, r1, r2, fp
    26→
    27→; ============================================================
    28→; Entry point
    29→; ============================================================
    30→_start:
    31→    ; Initialize VM state struct (fp = &vm_state)
    32→    la r0, vm_state
    33→    push r0
    34→    pop fp
    35→
    36→    ; pc = 0
    37→    lc r0, 0
    38→    sw r0, 0(fp)
    39→
    40→    ; esp = eval_stack base
    41→    la r0, eval_stack
    42→    sw r0, 3(fp)
    43→
    44→    ; csp = call_stack base
    45→    la r0, call_stack
    46→    sw r0, 6(fp)
    47→
    48→    ; fp_vm = 0 (no frame yet)
    49→    lc r0, 0
    50→    sw r0, 9(fp)
    51→
    52→    ; gp = globals base
    53→    la r0, globals_seg
    54→    sw r0, 12(fp)
    55→
    56→    ; hp = heap base
    57→    la r0, heap_seg
    58→    sw r0, 15(fp)
    59→
    60→    ; code = code segment base
    61→    la r0, code_seg
    62→    sw r0, 18(fp)
    63→
    64→    ; status = 0 (running)
    65→    lc r0, 0
    66→    sw r0, 21(fp)
    67→
    68→    ; trap_code = 0
    69→    lc r0, 0
    70→    sw r0, 24(fp)
    71→
    72→    ; Print boot message
    73→    la r0, msg_boot
    74→    la r2, uart_puts
    75→    jal r1, (r2)
    76→
    77→    ; Enter VM main loop
    78→    la r0, vm_loop
    79→    jmp (r0)
    80→
    81→; ============================================================
    82→; UART helpers
    83→; ============================================================
    84→
    85→; uart_putc — send byte in r0 to UART
    86→; Call via: jal r1, (r2) with r2 = uart_putc
    87→; Clobbers: r0, r2 (r1 = return address, preserved)
    88→uart_putc:
    89→    push r0
    90→    la r2, -65280
    91→uart_putc_wait:
    92→    lb r0, 1(r2)
    93→    cls r0, z
    94→    brt uart_putc_wait
    95→    pop r0
    96→    sb r0, 0(r2)
    97→    jmp (r1)
    98→
    99→; uart_puts — print null-terminated string at address in r0
   100→; Call via: jal r1, (r2) with r2 = uart_puts, r0 = string addr

file.content:
; pvm.s — P-Code Virtual Machine for COR24
;
; Register allocation:
;   r0 = W (work/scratch, opcode dispatch)
;   r1 = scratch / return address for jal calls
;   r2 = scratch
;   sp = COR24 hardware stack (EBR, used sparingly)
;   fp = memory base for indexed loads/stores
;
; VM state lives in memory struct (vm_state) because COR24 has only 3 GPRs.
;
; UART: data at -65280 (0xFF0100), status at -65279 (0xFF0101)
;   TX busy = status bit 7 (sign bit via lb sign-extend)
;   RX ready = status bit 0
; LED: port at -65024 (0xFF0200)
;
; COR24 ISA notes:
;   lbu = load byte zero-extend, lb = load byte sign-extend
;   ceq ra, rb sets C if ra == rb; cls ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (signed)
;   clu ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (unsigned)
;   brt/brf = branch if C true/false; bra = branch always
;   jal r1, (r2) = r1 = PC+1, PC = r2 (call convention)
;   jmp (r1) = return from jal call
;   Valid load/store base registers: r0, r1, r2, fp (NOT sp)
;   Can push/pop: r0, r1, r2, fp

; ============================================================
; Entry point
; ============================================================
_start:
    ; Initialize VM state struct (fp = &vm_state)
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp

    ; pc = 0
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 0(fp)

    ; esp = eval_stack base
    la r0, eval_stack
    sw r0, 3(fp)

    ; csp = call_stack base
    la r0, call_stack
    sw r0, 6(fp)

    ; fp_vm = 0 (no frame yet)
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 9(fp)

    ; gp = globals base
    la r0, globals_seg
    sw r0, 12(fp)

    ; hp = heap base
    la r0, heap_seg
    sw r0, 15(fp)

    ; code = code segment base
    la r0, code_seg
    sw r0, 18(fp)

    ; status = 0 (running)
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 21(fp)

    ; trap_code = 0
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 24(fp)

    ; Print boot message
    la r0, msg_boot
    la r2, uart_puts
    jal r1, (r2)

    ; Enter VM main loop
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; UART helpers
; ============================================================

; uart_putc — send byte in r0 to UART
; Call via: jal r1, (r2) with r2 = uart_putc
; Clobbers: r0, r2 (r1 = return address, preserved)
uart_putc:
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
uart_putc_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt uart_putc_wait
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    jmp (r1)

; uart_puts — print null-terminated string at address in r0
; Call via: jal r1, (r2) with r2 = uart_puts, r0 = string addr

; ============================================================

; 0x00 — halt: set status=1, return to vm_loop
op_halt:
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lc r0, 1
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x01 — push imm24: fetch 3-byte operand, push onto eval stack
op_push:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = &code[pc]
    lw r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = 24-bit immediate
    push r2
    ; Increment pc by 3
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 3
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Push r2 onto eval stack
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = esp
    pop r0

file.content:
la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lw r0, 21(fp)
    ceq r0, z
    brf vm_halted

    ; Fetch opcode byte from code[pc]
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = code_base + pc, use r0 as base register
    lbu r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = opcode byte (zero-extended)

    ; Increment pc
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)

    ; Bounds check: opcode must be < 97 (0x00..0x60)
    mov r0, r2
    lc r2, 97
    clu r0, r2
    brt opcode_ok
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)
opcode_ok:

    ; Dispatch: compute dispatch_table[opcode * 3]
    mov r2, r0
    add r0, r0
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = 3 * opcode
    la r2, dispatch_table
    add r2, r0
    ; r2 = &dispatch_table[opcode * 3]
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = handler address
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; VM halt / trap exit
; ============================================================
vm_halted:
    ; r0 = status (nonzero)
    ; Check if trapped (status == 2)
    lc r2, 2
    ceq r0, r2
    brt vm_trapped
    ; Normal halt
    la r0, msg_halted
    la r2, uart_puts_final
    jal r1, (r2)
vm_trapped:
    la r0, msg_trap
    la r2, uart_puts_final
    jal r1, (r2)

; uart_puts_final — print string then enter halt loop
; r0 = string address. Does not return.
uart_puts_final:
    mov r1, r0
uart_puts_final_loop:
    lbu r0, 0(r1)
    ceq r0, z
    brt halt_loop
    push r1
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
uart_puts_final_tx:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt uart_puts_final_tx
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    pop r1
    add r1, 1
    bra uart_puts_final_loop

halt_loop:
    bra halt_loop

; ============================================================
; Opcode handlers (all stubs for now)
; ============================================================

; 0x00 — halt: set status=1, return to vm_loop
op_halt:
    la r0, vm_state

1. Expand op_sys dispatch to handle IDs 0-5
2. Add handlers for HALT(0), GETC(2), LED(3), ALLOC(4), FREE(5)
3. Write test bytecode exercising the new syscalls

; 0x60 — sys id8: system call dispatch
op_sys:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    ; Fetch sys ID byte
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    lbu r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = sys id
    ; Increment pc
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Dispatch on sys id
    mov r0, r2
    lc r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2
    brt sys_putc
    ; Unknown sys id — trap
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)

; sys PUTC (id=1): pop char from eval stack, send to UART
sys_putc:
    ; fp = &vm_state
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    add r2, -3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = new esp = &TOS
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = char value
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
sys_putc_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt sys_putc_wait
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)
input.new_string:
; 0x60 — sys id8: system call dispatch
op_sys:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    ; Fetch sys ID byte
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    lbu r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = sys id
    ; Increment pc
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Dispatch on sys id (0=HALT, 1=PUTC, 2=GETC, 3=LED, 4=ALLOC, 5=FREE)
    mov r0, r2
    ceq r0, z
    brt sys_halt
    mov r0, r2
    lc r0, 1
    ceq r2, r0
    brt sys_putc
    mov r0, r2
    lc r0, 2
    ceq r2, r0
    brt sys_getc
    mov r0, r2
    lc r0, 3
    ceq r2, r0
    brt sys_led
    mov r0, r2
    lc r0, 4
    ceq r2, r0
    brt sys_alloc_j
    mov r0, r2
    lc r0, 5
    ceq r2, r0
    brt sys_free_j
    ; Unknown sys id — trap
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)

; Jump trampolines for far handlers
sys_alloc_j:
    la r0, sys_alloc
    jmp (r0)
sys_free_j:
    la r0, sys_free
    jmp (r0)

; sys HALT (id=0): stop VM execution
sys_halt:
    ; fp = &vm_state
    lc r0, 1
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; sys PUTC (id=1): pop char from eval stack, send to UART
sys_putc:
    ; fp = &vm_state
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    add r2, -3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = new esp = &TOS
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = char value
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
sys_putc_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt sys_putc_wait
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; sys GETC (id=2): read byte from UART, push onto eval stack
sys_getc:
    ; fp = &vm_state
    la r2, -65280
sys_getc_wait:
    lbu r0, 1(r2)
    ; bit 0 = RX ready
    lc r2, 1
    and r0, r2
    ceq r0, z
    brt sys_getc_wait
    ; RX ready — read data byte
    la r2, -65280
    lbu r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = received byte
    ; Push onto eval stack
    la r2, vm_state
    push r2
    pop fp
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = esp
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; sys LED (id=3): pop state from eval stack, write to LED port
sys_led:
    ; fp = &vm_state
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    add r2, -3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = new esp = &TOS
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = LED state
    la r2, -65024
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; sys ALLOC (id=4): pop size, bump-allocate, push pointer
sys_alloc:
    ; fp = &vm_state
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    ; Pop size from eval stack
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    add r2, -3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = new esp = &TOS
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = requested size (in bytes)
    ; Save size to temp
    push r0
    ; Get current heap pointer
    lw r0, 15(fp)
    ; r0 = hp (this is the allocated pointer)
    ; Save allocated pointer
    push r0
    ; Compute new hp = hp + size
    ; Load size from stack (second item)
    pop r2
    ; r2 = hp (allocated ptr)
    pop r0
    ; r0 = size
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = new hp
    ; Store new hp
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    ; Recover: we need hp and new_hp
    ; Let's redo this more carefully with temps
    ; -- restart with temp storage --
    ; Use nonlocal_temps for scratch
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lw r0, 15(fp)
    ; r0 = current hp (= allocated pointer)
    la r2, nonlocal_temps
    push r2
    pop fp
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; nonlocal_temps[0] = allocated ptr (old hp)
    ; Now get size from eval stack (already popped above... )
    ; Actually let me rewrite this handler cleanly
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)
